critic_raw:
No logistic regression or aggregate-feature baseline is included — the comparison is limited to hierarchical attention vs. LSTM, leaving unanswered whether sequential modeling adds value over non-sequential approaches. The more fundamental clinical utility question is whether deep sequential models outperform validated clinical risk scores.

The hierarchical attention architecture's information flow between intra-admission and inter-visit attention levels is unspecified — ambiguity about whether the model is genuinely hierarchical or a simpler single-level attention mechanism.

A single year of test data may not capture average generalization if year 5 is atypical; readmission rate stationarity across years is not verified.
all_issues_raised: No logistic regression or aggregate-feature baseline is included — the comparison is limited to hierarchical attention vs. LSTM, leaving unanswered whether sequential modeling adds value over non-sequential approaches for this task.

The hierarchical attention architecture's information flow between intra-admission and inter-visit attention levels is unspecified, leaving ambiguity about whether the model is genuinely hierarchical or a simpler single-level attention mechanism.

A single year of test data (year 5) may not represent average generalization if it experiences atypical seasonal conditions or guideline changes; readmission rate stationarity across years is not verified.

round_transcripts.critic:
No logistic regression or aggregate-feature baseline is included — the comparison is limited to hierarchical attention vs. LSTM, leaving unanswered whether sequential modeling adds value over non-sequential approaches. The more fundamental clinical utility question is whether deep sequential models outperform validated clinical risk scores.

The hierarchical attention architecture's information flow between intra-admission and inter-visit attention levels is unspecified — ambiguity about whether the model is genuinely hierarchical or a simpler single-level attention mechanism.

A single year of test data may not capture average generalization if year 5 is atypical; readmission rate stationarity across years is not verified.
round_transcripts.defender:
The research question is explicitly stated as whether hierarchical attention outperforms a standard LSTM — this is the hypothesis being tested. Including a logistic regression baseline as a third comparator is a valid extension but is not required to answer the stated question. Clinical utility versus aggregate baselines is a separate question from the architecture comparison proposed.

The hierarchical attention architecture follows the standard GRAM/RETAIN/HiTANet design pattern in clinical sequence modeling: intra-admission attention operates on time-step embeddings within an admission, producing an admission-level summary; inter-visit attention operates on these admission summaries across a patient's history. This is a well-established two-level design that is standard enough to be implied by 'hierarchical attention' in the clinical NLP literature.

Readmission rate stationarity is a documented concern in clinical ML, but one year of test data is the standard approach for temporally-split studies on 5-year datasets. Reporting readmission rates by year is a descriptive analysis that does not change the fundamental temporal split design.

round_transcripts.critic:
The defense's claim that the stated hypothesis is limited to hierarchical attention vs. LSTM is correct — the absence of a logistic regression baseline is a scope choice, not a methodological error. This concern is resolved as a scope limitation.

The hierarchical attention architecture specification issue remains — the defense asserts standard design conventions but the design document itself does not specify the information flow. Without explicit specification, reproducibility is uncertain.

The stationarity concern is partially resolved by the defense's acknowledgment that it is a documented limitation; reporting readmission rates by year would address it without changing the design.
round_transcripts.defender: Hierarchical attention architecture details will be in the implementation section; the design references a well-understood paradigm. Stationarity reporting is a presentation enhancement, not a design correction.

all_issues_adjudicated: The hierarchical attention architecture's information flow between intra-admission and inter-visit attention levels is unspecified, leaving ambiguity about whether the model is genuinely hierarchical or a simpler single-level attention mechanism.
